instruction_overrides:
def make_alu_r_swapped(spec, mnemonic):
    rd = Operand("rd", RiscObfRegister, write=True)
    rn = Operand("rn", RiscObfRegister, read=True)
    rm = Operand("rm", RiscObfRegister, read=True)
    syntax = Syntax([mnemonic, " ", rd, ",", " ", rn, ",", " ", rm])

    patterns = {
        "rs1": rm,
        "rs2": rn,
        "rd": rd
    }
    
    members = {
        "syntax": syntax,
        "rd": rd,
        "rn": rn,
        "rm": rm,
        "operand_patterns": patterns,
        "spec_key": spec
    }

    name = mnemonic.title() + "RegRegReg"
    return type(name, (RiscObfInstructionNew,), members)

Slt = make_alu_r_swapped("slt", "slt")
Subr = make_alu_r_swapped("subr", "sub")
Sltu = make_alu_r_swapped("sltu", "sltu")

Blt = make_branch("blt", "blt", True)
Bge = make_branch("bge", "bge", True)
Bltu = make_branch("bltu", "bltu", True)
Bgeu = make_branch("bgeu", "bgeu", True)

# These are effectively pseudo instructions created by inverting the operands
Bgt = make_branch("blt", "bgt", False)
Ble = make_branch("bge", "ble", False)
Bgtu = make_branch("bltu", "bgtu", False)
Bleu = make_branch("bgeu", "bleu", False)
